element_choice.justification: Every element is correctly chosen: <article> for the main article and cards/comments, <time datetime> for all dates, <pre><code> for the code block, <aside> for the pull quote (no <blockquote>), <strong> for comment author names, <footer> within <article> for the author bio, and <button type='submit'>. No div/span used where semantic elements exist.

aria_discipline.justification: ARIA is used sparingly and correctly: <nav aria-label='Main'>, <aside aria-label='Pull quote'>, and all <section> elements carry aria-labelledby pointing to their headings. No redundant ARIA (no role='button' on buttons, no role='navigation' on nav). tabindex='-1' on <main> is appropriate to support the skip link.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Single <h1> for the article title; <h2> for all article sections, author bio, Related Articles, and Comments; <h3> for related article card titles — all correct. Minor issue: the 'Leave a Comment' section uses <h2> while being structurally nested inside the <section> headed by the 'Comments' <h2>, making it a sibling heading level inside a child section rather than a subordinate <h3>.

landmark_structure.justification: Skip link is the first focusable element targeting <main id='main-content'>. <header>, <nav aria-label='Main'>, <main>, <aside>, and <footer> are all used correctly. Site header and footer are outside <main>. All <section> elements are labelled with aria-labelledby. <footer> within <article> correctly scopes the author bio.

form_semantics.justification: All inputs and the textarea have associated <label> elements with matching for/id pairs; appropriate input types (text, email) are used; <button type='submit'> is correct. No <fieldset>/<legend> wrapping the form group, and no error-handling pattern (aria-invalid, aria-describedby) is present, which are minor omissions.

content_realism.justification: All content is realistic and specific: concrete statistics (120 endpoints, 38% load time reduction, 52% payload decrease), plausible author bios, varied commenter names, and a real-looking GraphQL query example. No lorem ipsum or placeholder text anywhere.

list_semantics.justification: Navigation items are in <ul> within <nav>, and the three related article cards are wrapped in a <ul> with each card as an <li>. List types are appropriate and count-relevant collections are consistently marked up as lists.

expectations_met: <main> wraps the article, related articles, and comments — site header and footer are outside it
Skip navigation link is the first focusable element ('Skip to main content')
Article sections use <h2> headings within the article
Related articles section has an <h2> heading and cards in a <ul>
Comment form uses proper <label> elements for all fields
Comment author names use <strong>, dates use <time>
<address> element is not used (correctly absent — no contact info context)
Heading hierarchy: h1 for article title, h2 for sections, h2 for Related Articles, h2 for Comments
expectations_missed: Comment form lacks <fieldset>/<legend> grouping — the expectation lists this as preferred over labels-only
'Leave a Comment' uses <h2> while nested inside the 'Comments' <h2> section, making it a same-level sibling heading in a child section rather than the expected <h3>
additional_observations: All <section> elements are labelled with aria-labelledby, which elevates them to proper landmarks — good discipline
The GraphQL code block is a realistic, syntactically correct query that reinforces content realism
